QF.id: I Codes of Conduct and Investor Protection_36

question.text: Under the ODR framework, an MII review of a complaint/dispute must be concluded within how many calendar days before referring the matter to an ODR Institution?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text states: "References to ODR Institutions shall be made after a review of such complaint/dispute by the relevant MII with the aim of amicable resolution and such review shall be concluded within 21 calendar days." **The Trap:** 21 calendar days appears in two places — MII review (before ODR Institution referral) AND conciliation (after ODR Institution appointment). Candidates can confuse the two 21-day periods. **Cross-Exam Context:** The ODR dispute resolution sequence: MII review (21 days) → ODR Institution appoints conciliator (5 days) → Conciliation (21 days, extendable by 10 days) → If failed, arbitration (30 days for award). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Two separate 21-day windows: (1) MII internal review before referral, and (2) Conciliation after appointment. Both are 21 days but in different phases.
